'''Theerapol Ritson''' (born 7 January 1996) is a [[Thailand|Thai]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]]

He previously played for [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]], [[Workington Town]], [[Newcastle Thunder]] and [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]]. Ritson has also appeared for the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship]], with St Helens having a [[RFL dual registration|dual-registration]] agreement in place at the time.

==Background==
Ritson was born in Thailand, but was raised in [[Maryport]], [[Cumbria]], England.

==Playing career==
===Workington Town===
His playing career started at [[Workington Town]] where he made 39 appearances between 2014 and 2017 scoring 12 tries.<ref name="LRL081122">{{cite news |title=St Helens swoop for sought-after Championship star Tee Ritson |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tee-ritson-st-helens-barrow-raiders |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=8 November 2022 |language=en}}</ref>

In 2016 he was the club's player of the year and was selected for the {{rlnt|Thailand}} national team.<ref>{{cite news |title=Young Tee's the talk of the Town |url=https://www.whitehavennews.co.uk/sport/17116994.young-tees-the-talk-of-the-town/ |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=Whitehaven News |date=26 September 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Workington Town winger Theerapol Ritson earns first international call-up for Thailand |url=https://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/16759877.workington-town-winger-theerapol-ritson-earns-first-international-call-up-for-thailand/ |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=News and Star |date=5 September 2016}}</ref> An international debut was missed as the game against {{rlnt|Malta}} was cancelled due to the death of King [[Bhumibol Adulyadej]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Malta tour of Thailand cancelled |url=https://staging.asiapacificrl.com/2016/10/17/malta-tour-thailand-cancelled/ |publisher=Asia Pacific Rugby League |access-date=28 July 2023 |date=17 October 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220630012609/https://staging.asiapacificrl.com/2016/10/17/malta-tour-thailand-cancelled/ |archive-date=30 June 2022}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder (loan)===
At the start of the [[2018 RFL League 1|2018]] season Ritson moved to Newcastle Thunder on loan where he made 22 appearances scoring 18 tries.<ref name="LRL081122"/>

===Barrow Raiders===
2019 saw a move to his home town club, Barrow Raiders, where he became a prolific try scorer over the next four seasons, scoring 70 tries in just 73 appearances.<ref name="LRL081122"/>  In 2022 he scored 33 tries in 31 games becoming the [[2022 RFL Championship|Championship]] leading try scorer for the season.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=Barrow Raiders star Tee Ritson makes Super League move |url=https://www.totalrl.com/barrow-raiders-star-tee-ritson-makes-super-league-move/ |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=TotalRL.com |date=8 November 2022}}</ref>

===Cumbria===
At the end of the 2022 season Ritson made his representative debut as he was selected to play for [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] in a [[2021 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] warm-up match against {{rlnt|Jamaica}}. Cumbria won 28–2 with Ritson scoring one of the Cumbrian's tries.<ref>{{cite news |title=Jamaica suffered defeat in Cumbria in World Cup warm-up |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamaica-suffered-defeat-in-cumbria-in-world-cup-warm-up |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=8 October 2022}}</ref>

===St Helens===
In November 2022, Super League side, St Helens, signed Ritson on a one-year loan deal.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Pattison |first1=Cam |title=Tee Ritson reveals why he chose St Helens and the St Helens legend he looked up to |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/tee-ritson-reveals-why-he-chose-st-helens-and-the-st-helens-legend-he-looked-up-to-61935/ |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=Rugby League News |date=8 November 2022}}</ref>
Ritson's debut for St Helens was in the warm-up match for the [[2023 World Club Challenge]] against [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] where he also scored his first try for St Helens in a 30–12 victory.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=St Helens overcome St George Illawarra Dragons in entertaining prelude to World Club Challenge |url=https://www.totalrl.com/st-helens-overcome-st-george-illawarra-dragons-in-entertaining-prelude-to-world-club-challenge/ |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=TotalRL.com |date=11 February 2023}}</ref>
His Super League debut came in St Helens 2023 opening fixture against [[Castleford Tigers]] where he scored his first Super League try in St Helens 24–6 win.<ref>{{cite news |title=Flying wing Tee Ritson is grabbing his Saints chance with both hands |url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23562345.tee-ritson-grabbing-saints-chance-hands/ |access-date=28 July 2023 |work=St Helens Star |date=1 June 2023}}</ref>
On 18 October 2023 it was reported that he had made his initial loan deal switch to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] permanent signing a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=18 Oct 2023|title=Tee Ritson: St Helens turn winger's loan switch from Barrow into permanent two-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67143672}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders (loan)===
On 28 February 2025 he signed for Barrow in the RFL Championship on a short-term two-week loan. On 4 September 2025, it was announced that Ritson had signed a two-year deal to return to Barrow on a permanent basis starting in 2026.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=28 Feb 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=St Helens winger Tee Ritson makes loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/st-helens-winger-tee-ritson-makes-loan-move/}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c9qnggx7qxzo|title=Ritson to make permanent Barrow return in 2026|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders (re-join)===
On 2 September 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Barrow Raiders|date=2 Sep 2025|accessdate=20 Dec 2025|title=Tee Ritson makes Raiders return on two-year deal|url=https://www.barrowrlfc.com/latest-news/tee-ritson-makes-raiders-return-on-two-year-deal/}}</ref>
